title: Erdős hub gap map (Records lane)
description: One record per bounded quantity attached to a hub problem: its known [lower, upper] state, what a WITNESS is, how to verify one cheaply, and honest feasibility grades. This is the Records-lane overlay: most open problems cannot be solved exactly by machine, but every bounded quantity with a gap accepts a better witness, and witnesses are cheaply verifiable. Curated + agent-mined; keyed on the canonical erdosproblems.com id; lives BESIDE compiler-generated stubs.json (never inside it). Numeric claims MUST carry a source; entries without verified numbers belong in the miner's skipped list, not here.

problem.description: canonical erdosproblems.com id (must exist in stubs.json)

quantity.description: human name of the bounded quantity, e.g. 'R(3,3,3,3)'

kind.description: value_gap = both bounds known; next_cell = first uncomputed table cell; verified_range = conjecture checked for all n <= lower.value

witness_side.description: which bound a constructive witness IMPROVES. Max-type quantity: a bigger example raises lower. Min-type quantity: a smaller example lowers upper.

witness_verifier.description: how to check a claimed witness cheaply (dependency-free sketch), or why no cheap check exists

witness_feasibility.description: open-easy = our bounded-search tools plausibly move it in hours; plausible = days or a clever encoding; hard = decades-open / expert tooling defeated, but witness still verifiable; historic = 50-year famous target; none = no cheap witness verification

exact_feasibility.description: can the EXACT value fall: cell = bounded exhaustive + cheap verify; drat-candidate = nonexistence side plausibly machine-certifiable (DRAT); wall = no known path

checked.description: sources actually consulted for the numbers (OEIS entries, surveys, arXiv ids)

evidence.description: WS7 epistemic ledger: the recorded evidence artifacts behind this entry's numbers. The confidence class is COMPUTED from this list by tools/validate_gap_map.py — never assigned by hand.

type.description: formal_proof = machine-checked formal proof; implementation = a verified implementation replicating the claim at its full range; replay_receipt = an in-repo certificate whose verifier replays the claim; numeric_scan = supporting numerics/cross-checks below full-replication grade; literature = numbers transcribed from published sources

artifact.description: what to look at: a repo path, a replay command, or a citation. Distinctness of this string is what makes two implementation/replay_receipt items count as independent for C1.

confidence.description: mechanical confidence class, derivable from evidence[] (validator recomputes and fails on mismatch): C0 = any formal_proof; C1 = >=2 implementation/replay_receipt items with distinct artifacts; C2 = exactly 1 such artifact; C3 = numeric_scan/literature only (conjecture-grade or literature-transcribed)

value.description: string to allow big integers and qualified values like '> 2×10^12'
